channel.title: Posts on Projects
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Projects

item.title: You Gotta Feel It
item.description:
Manipulation is fundamentally the exchange of forces between the manipulator and manipulated to change the state. This “state” could be a variety of things: position, velocity, orientation and additional internal configurations (like opening a book or unscrewing a bottle).

In robotics the current belle of the ball for manipulation is the VLA (vision-language-action) policy: image and language conditioned model that generates joint trajectories. And these are popular for good reason, they’ve initiated a step function in manipulation performance and offer alternatives to previous brittle, manually engineered task policies.

item.title: Levers, Bicycles, and Robots
item.description:
Give me a place to stand, and a lever long enough, and I will move the world

– Archimedes

I’ve spent the better part of my adult life (not much considering I’m 23) trying to figure out how to get robots into peoples lives. Sure, there are technically robotic systems in your house thermostats and the like, but the only “true” robot that has seen large adoption is a little hockey puck that vacuums and/or mops your floors. Why haven’t we seen more robots? Where is R2? Where is Rosey and The Doctor’s K9?

item.title: DDLabs
item.description: I built a 4 quadrant dynamometer (essentially a treadmill for cars) to test the performance of DoorDash lab’s indevelopment autonomous delivery robots. I worked on electrical design, building the platform from first principles to be able to simulate different loads and road conditions for development and repair validation. I also developed software and firware to drive the brushless motor we used to drive the dyno and to simulate different loads. The following year, I focused on developing controls to allow the motor to accurately follow torque and velocity profiles. I also built an interface for non-technical personnel to use the dyno for testing.

item.title: Using Latex in Hugo
item.description:
For my last post I added some equations using a math formatting tool called LaTex. This will NOT be an introduction to LaTex but will explain a bit about how I integrated it into my Hugo site. I was having a bit of trouble getting everything to work, so I figured it would be convenient for other people if this little walkthrough existed.

This site is built on a custom Hugo theme and hosted on GitHub. I have it connected to a Namecheap domain. Hugo is a framework that simplifies the process of generating static content. You can find out more about the hosting and Hugo here and here respectively.

item.title: Letters and Knowledge
item.description:
(While there is definitely somethings to say about letters like grades and degrees and how that correlates to knowledge but I haven’t fleshed that out yet, so stay tuned)

I ended my Feynman post talking about how I wanted to be a polymath, and I think that idea is something that has informed a lot of decisions I’ve made in my life.

Let me back up a bit. My freshman year of college, I tried to make my own major. I was, and still am, very interested in robotics. I got in for mechanical engineering and that is what I eventually studied, along with a minor in Robotics, but I wanted to be able to blend the various disciplines involved in robotics into a single course scheduele. Ultimately, this didn’t come to fruition but it was a recurring debate with my roommate over the next few years as to whether or not it had merits.

item.title: On Richard Feynman and the "Build in Public" movement
Keyboard Update II - LVGL and Shift Registers
item.description: Second development log on the keyboard

item.title: Phlebot

item.title: Back to the keyboard!
item.description:
Back in 2021 I made a seven key macropad with an encoder knob using QMK. The original goal with that project was to serve as a trial case for building a fullsized (or at least 65%) keyboard. Two years later, I’m finally getting around to it. I took so long because I was

The design I’m going with is largely inspired by a collection of tweets by Claudio Guglieri.

Here are a couple:

More keyboard renders with touch display and led keycaps. pic.twitter.com/lJBygeG31V
